title: toxicodendron zeyheri
intro: Toxicodendron zeyheri, also known as the South African poison ivy or poison oak, is a plant species within the Anacardiaceae family that is native to South Africa. The plant contains urushiol, a toxic oil that causes severe skin irritation and allergic reactions in humans. Despite its toxic properties, Toxicodendron zeyheri has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It can be prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, decoctions, and topical ointments, which are said to have benefits for skin conditions, wounds, and infections.
